text:
17.6     Where a DAPO is served on the person to be protected (and that person is not the applicant), it must be accompanied by a notice informing that person—


(a)        how to—


(i)         apply to be joined as a party to the proceedings; and


(ii)        make an application to vary or discharge the DAPO; and


(b)        of that person’s right to make representations orally or in writing at any subsequent hearing.


17.7     Where the DAPO relates to a property and details of any mortgagee or landlord of the property were set out in the DAPO application pursuant to paragraph 3.4(c)(ii), the applicant must serve a copy of the DAPO on the mortgagee or landlord of the property.


17.8     A certificate of service stating the date and time of service on each person served must be filed by—


(a)        the person or organisation who served the DAPO and any other documents; or


(b)        the representative of the person or organisation.


17.9     This paragraph applies to service of a DAPO varied or discharged by the court on notice as it applies to service of a DAPO made by the court on notice.


## 18. Service of DAPO made without notice to P


18.1     Where the court makes a DAPO without notice to P, the applicant must serve the persons listed in paragraph 17.1, as soon as reasonably practicable, with the following documents—


(a)        a copy of the DAPO;


(b)        a copy of the application for the DAPO together with supporting statement; and


(c)        if required by paragraph 17.6, the notice of information for the person to be protected,


in accordance with the relevant provisions of paragraph 17.


18.2     Where the DAPO relates to a property and details of any mortgagee or landlord of the property were set out in the DAPO application pursuant to paragraph 3.4(c)(ii), the applicant must serve a copy of the DAPO on the mortgagee or landlord of the property.


18.3     Rule 27.2 FPR (reasons for a decision: proceedings before a lay justice or justices) does not apply.


18.4     This paragraph applies to service of a DAPO varied or discharged by the court without notice as it applies to service of a DAPO made by the court without notice.
